summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a strong set with standout Miracle > Shakedown, Terrapin and Jack Straw performances.
review: The matrix source is praised for its vivid presence, delivering crystal‑clear sound that lets the band’s interplay shine.  Highlights include an epic early‑Miracle jam that flows into Shakedown Street, a powerful Terrapin, and a second‑solo‑laden Jack Straw, while the whole show feels solid despite not being the Dead at their absolute peak.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are favorable (4‑5 stars).  Show quality is described as strong with several standout songs, and the matrix recording is repeatedly called “great” and “alive,” justifying a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note the matrix mix brings the performance to life; no complaints about drop‑outs, muddiness, or imbalance.  Sound is described as crisp and vivid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows well, with early Miracle jams leading into Shakedown Street and a powerful Terrapin later; reviewers appreciate the new‑progression feel even if Keith’s playing is less interactive than usual.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mentioned as part of the group channeling new progressions in old standards.
band_member_comments.Phil: His onstage banter is called “hilarious,” adding extra flavor to the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Cited alongside Jerry, Phil, Bill and Mickey for pushing forward fresh ideas within familiar songs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard work is noted as less interactive than his best moments but still supportive of the evolving sound.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey are praised for channeling new progressions with their drumming.
